classObjective: Recognize when to multiply one equation versus both equations to create opposite coefficients and evaluate and compare solution methods for systems of linear equations.
workedExamples: 7-4, Example 2 — Applications of Systems of Equations (continued)
warmUp: Compare the complexity of eliminating x from 3x + 4y = 2 and 5x + 8y = 6 vs. 6x + 8y = 4 and 5x + 8y = 6
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: least common multiple strategy; method comparison arguments; efficiency analysis
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice choosing optimal elimination strategy and comparing solution methods
independentPractice: Practice problems evaluating elimination strategies and comparing solution approaches
assessment: Exit ticket: solve 3x + 2y = 12 and 5x - 3y = 7 using the most efficient elimination approach
capReflection: How do you determine whether to multiply one equation or both when setting up elimination?
